programmes.show_description: Sketch covers a shoddy Egyptian-themed tattoo for Ste. Dave's dragged to the studio by his son and daughter, who can't bear his obscene back-piece any longer.
Jo Brand demonstrates an impressive grasp of street slang and David Baddiel takes a sledgehammer approach. Former series champion Katherine Ryan returns.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Lee Mack and Victoria Coren Mitchell take on Alan Carr and Dane Baptiste. Sam Simmons joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Teleshopping
Teleshopping
Mitchell Modell, CEO of Modell's Sporting Goods, America's oldest family-owned company selling athletics apparel and equipment, goes incognito in his own firm.
Kat Cole, the president of Cinnabon Incorporated, which serves sweet treats from over 900 bakeries in 40 countries, goes incognito among her own workforce.
In Swansea and Bridgend, the competition includes terrible table manners and tactless banter, chicken lettuce boats, kitchen chaos - and a final showdown at the dinner table.
In Cumbria, Sarka and Paul's massive Czech portions defeat almost all of their guests. Plus: there's a musical extravaganza, and a menu that takes a tour of Europe.
...Selling: New parents Ela and Noam are looking for a family home. First the twins must fashion their walk-in wardrobe into a bedroom. Can they give their home a stylish makeover?
The One in an Aquarium: Essex groom Gary plans an aquatic wedding 200 miles from home. But Lauren hopes for a home counties service. Will Gary's underwater dream sink without a trace?
The Oktoberfest One: Sofia dreams of a boho beach blessing, but Craig wants an Oktoberfest wedding with short leather trousers. Will Sofia end the nuptials?
John Wilson, the president and chief operating officer of Rollins Incorporated - the parent company of eight pest control firms - goes incognito to work among his employees.
Randy DeWitt, the CEO of sports bar and grill chain Twin Peaks, goes incognito among his own staff, to find out if the company's culture is keeping up with its rapid growth.
River Rock Inn: Gordon checks into the River Rock Inn in Pennsylvania, where owner Ken Pisciotta, who's drowning in debt, can't convince guests to stay in his neglected hotel.
The Keating: Gordon checks into The Keating, a boutique hotel in San Diego, California. Its owner has blown millions on design but hasn't spent on items the guests really need.
...and Selling: Brand new series: Neither Madan nor Simone are willing to budge on their wish list. Can Drew break the deadlock? Jonathan wields power tools as he destroys décor.
Mer-Made a Big Mis-Cake: Daisy summons a mermaid from the sea, but happily-ever-after isn't on the cards as a cake mishap jeopardises the whole evening.
A throwing task leaves some of the contestants with egg on their faces. Jo Brand reveals her stunning psychic superpowers. Katy Wix creates 'Mash Rushmore'.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Katherine Ryan take on Jon Richardson and Joe Wilkinson. Dr John Cooper Clarke joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Rob Beckett take on Jon Richardson and Claudia Winkleman. Plus: special guests Nick Mohammed and Joe Wilkinson join Susie Dent and Rachel Riley.
The Violin: Adam isn't happy when he is forced to play his violin for Mum and her best friend Val, but Jonny is relishing every moment. Dad is obsessed with his old microscope.
Wilson: The boys are threatened with no crumble if they don't help to assemble a wooden shelf, while Jim's dog Wilson keeps doing his 'dollops' in Mum and Dad's flowerbed.
Jay helps to cover paramedic Anthony's tattoo, which is putting the frighteners on his patients, while Sketch deals with Alex's drunken DIY teddy bear design.
A throwing task leaves some of the contestants with egg on their faces. Jo Brand reveals her stunning psychic superpowers. Katy Wix creates 'Mash Rushmore'.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Katherine Ryan take on Jon Richardson and Joe Wilkinson. Dr John Cooper Clarke joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Teleshopping
Teleshopping
Stephen J Cloobeck, CEO of hotel firm Diamond Resorts International, is the first boss to go undercover in his business for a second time, as he visits Pacific Monarch Resorts.
Steve Greenbaum, co-founder and CEO of PostNet, which helps small businesses with everything from printing to design, goes undercover to see how well his firm is functioning.
In Blackpool, Antonio and ex-redcoat Jason serve controversial sushi, there's an eyebrow-raising discovery at firefighter Daz and Kim's place, and foodies Martin and Kate serve Thai treats.
In Swindon, fashion-conscious Carla and Hayley serve up a spicy, Caribbean-themed menu to self-confessed food snob Jeanette and her husband Rob, and lovebirds Tim and Kath.
...Selling: Daniel and Vanessa get hands-on as they transform their lacklustre home into a sale-ready house. But can Drew balance glitz and glam with a macho 'man cave'?
The One with the Party Boy Groom: Reluctant Lee devises Las Vegas-style nuptials, but it's not the family church in Norfolk that his bride Anna had in mind.
The One with the Groom Hunt: Groom Lee and his brother take off on a tour of Europe, telling bride Becky that if she wants to marry Lee, she has to find him first.
Steven Newman, CEO of Loehmann's department stores, goes undercover on the front line of his company, to ensure the outlets are implementing his plans to reinvigorate the brand.
Jane Grote Abell, the chairwoman of Donatos Pizza and daughter of the company's founder, goes undercover to ensure that her father's values are still being implemented.
Meson de Mesilla: Can Gordon Ramsay fix Meson de Mesilla, a Tuscan-style hotel in Las Cruces, New Mexico, that's falling flat?
The Monticello Hotel: Gordon visits the struggling Monticello Hotel in Longview, Washington. Can he keep the doors to this 90-year-old establishment open and save 50 staff jobs?
...and Selling: Brand new series: Jonie was joyful when her daughter Taisha moved back in, despite the lack of space. Now Jonathan and Drew must make their home look brand new.
Bean Counter: Dr Tiffany Moon, her husband Daniel and their friends step aboard, hoping for a luxurious five-star experience. Will they be left wanting more?
Greg Davies delivers angry appraisals from his comfy throne. This time: bins, balloons and a big baby. Plus: putting lyrics to the Taskmaster theme tune.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Phil Wang take on guest captain Alan Carr and Harriet Kemsley. David O'Doherty and Joe Wilkinson join Susie Dent and Rachel Riley.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Richard Ayoade take on Jon Richardson and Jessica Knappett. Alex Horne and The Horne Section join Susie Dent. With Rachel Riley.
The Caravan: Robert Popper's comedy about the Goodman family. Dad's bought a grotty caravan. Jim's got a new dog. And the boys have new 'females' but can they bear to tell Mum?
The Plastic Bag: Dad's obsessed with retrieving a plastic bag from a tree. Jonny's sense of style is out of this world. And Adam discovers hell hath no fury like his Mum scorned.
